text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. It focuses on breaking work into small, manageable cycles called sprints, which usually last one to four weeks. At the start, the team creates a backlog, a list of tasks or features to complete. Each task is described as a user story, which explains a feature from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and plan the day. This iterative development process allows teams to adapt quickly to feedback and deliver improvements regularly.
